name: North-South Onion Pancakes
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Cold water (add more if dough feels too dry)
recipeInstructions:
--------------------------
Filling:
3 ounces Boneless, skinless chicken breast, diced
1/2 teaspoon Cornstarch
1/4 teaspoon Cooking sherry
1/4 pound Chinese sausage, diced
1 tablespoon Chinese dried shrimp, minced
1 teaspoon Vegetable oil
3 ounces Chinese barbecued pork, diced
1 bunch Green onions, finely chopped
1 teaspoon Oyster sauce (optional)
1 teaspoon Soy sauce
1/4 teaspoon Sugar
1/4 teaspoon Salt
1. Measure the flour into a large mixing bowl. Add boiling water and stir quickly with chopsticks or fork until smooth. Gradually add cold water and mix well until dough is smooth and supple.
2. Roll the dough into a ball, cover with plastic wrap or damp cloth, and let rest for 1 hour at room temperature.
3. In a small bowl, combine chicken with cornstarch, cooking sherry, and pinch of salt. Refrigerate until ready to use.
4. Heat vegetable oil in a small frying pan over high heat. Stir-fry Chinese sausage and dried shrimp for about 1 minute. Add the chicken with its marinade and stir-fry for another 2 minutes. Quickly stir in barbecued pork and minced onions (reserve some for pancake wrappers). Season with oyster sauce, soy sauce, sugar, and salt.
5. Turn off heat; divide filling into 16 portions and set aside.
6. Knead the dough until smooth and divide equally into 16 pieces. Cover each piece with plastic wrap.
7. Work one piece of dough at a time, keeping the rest covered in plastic wrap. Rub both sides of the dough with shortening (about 1 teaspoon), sprinkle evenly with minced scallions and salt.
8. Curl up the dough from one end into a long, narrow cylinder, then curl it again lengthwise into a snail-like cylinder. Place the dough on its broad side and roll out again with a rolling pin into a piece about 4 inches in diameter.
9. Place one portion of filling in the center of the dough and seal well by pinching the dough like a dumpling. Flatten into a 2-inch-diameter onion pancake. Repeat until all dough is prepared.
10. Pan fry the onion pancakes in hot vegetable oil over medium heat, about 2 minutes on each side or until golden brown. Pat the onion pancakes gently with paper towels to remove excess oil before serving. Makes 16 Pancakes, or 8 Servings
